h2.note: transcript-level proxy for the per-turn H2 (§4.3); needs a real (non-mock) full-N run to mean anything

cliIsolation.claude: non-persistent temp cwd with project/local settings and tools disabled

scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner continues following the tutor's spatial placement and labeling procedure without any departure from the expected forward sequence.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner merely follows the tutor's step-by-step placement and recall instructions throughout, without performing any new task or device that shows the learning problem has moved beyond the initial setup.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutor smoothly increments the same spatial labeling routine without any stock-taking contrast or new adaptive mechanism.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is introduced; the tutor consistently applies the same tile-labelling procedure.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: There is no pivot learner turn triggering a revised learner framing, so no later tutor adaptation to such a frame occurs.

learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner merely follows the tutor's step-by-step placement and recall instructions throughout, without performing any new task or device that shows the learning problem has moved beyond the initial setup.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: There is no pivot learner turn triggering a revised learner framing, so no later tutor adaptation to such a frame occurs.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutor smoothly increments the same spatial labeling routine without any stock-taking contrast or new adaptive mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutor smoothly increments the same spatial labeling routine without any stock-taking contrast or new adaptive mechanism.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is introduced; the tutor consistently applies the same tile-labelling procedure.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Friction is table on cart too, but not the same table mark as normal.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: The two table cards need their force names first, or they look too much alike.
scored.naiveTrajectory: The learner would continue to place cards and name forces as instructed, focusing on contact marks.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The two table cards need their force names first, or they look too much alike.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: The learner introduces a new sorting criterion (force names first) that goes beyond the tutor's instructions and shows productive action.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutor's strategy remains consistent and unchallenged.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: No adaptive mechanism is introduced by the tutor.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: No tutor turn follows the pivot learner turn, so no contingent adaptation is possible.

learner_self_reframe.evidence: Friction is table on cart too, but not the same table mark as normal.

learner_actional_breakthrough.evidence: The two table cards need their force names first, or they look too much alike.
learner_actional_breakthrough.justification: The learner introduces a new sorting criterion (force names first) that goes beyond the tutor's instructions and shows productive action.

tutor_contingent_adaptation.justification: No tutor turn follows the pivot learner turn, so no contingent adaptation is possible.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutor's strategy remains consistent and unchallenged.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism.justification: No learner turn creates pressure for a reversal; the tutor's strategy remains consistent and unchallenged.

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: No adaptive mechanism is introduced by the tutor.

scored.recoheredEarlier: Top box: three beats. Bottom box: the quarter-note tile gets one beat. The 4 still pulls toward speed for me, so I want to test it by putting the bar line after the third tile.
scored.statedInsightEvidence: so here the 4 is naming that unit, not setting speed
scored.naiveTrajectory: L1 asks to test 'the 4 still pulls toward speed for me' by setting a bar line, expecting the test to confirm or deny a speed interpretation; L2 should report what the bar line shows about speed.
scored.actionalBreakthroughEvidence: The bar ends there: three eighth-note beat units make one bar in 3/8. The quarter-note tile covers two eighth-note spaces, so the remaining single eighth-note card fills the gap. Next check: a dotted quarter should cover all three eighth-note units.
scored.actionalBreakthroughJustification: In L4 the learner performs a new classification and a criterion-transfer task — counting eighth-note beat units for a new time signature and extending the reasoning to predict a dotted quarter — moving beyond the original quarter-note context without tutor prompting.
scored.tutorReversalEvidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
scored.tutorReversalJustification: In T2 the tutor shows stock-taking contrast by explicitly replacing 'which box did the bar line test' with 'Read the lower box first' and by ruling out speed-language, then introduces a new public mechanism: a changed object (eighth-note card and 8 in the lower box) and a shifted task (identify the beat unit from the lower number, then re-read the quarter-note as covering a count of that unit rather than setting speed).
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityEvidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
scored.adaptiveMechanismQualityJustification: The mechanism is precise and directly fitted to the learner's pressure on the lower number: it makes the beat unit a physical tile the learner selects, reframes the quarter-note as a tile that covers n beat-units rather than a speed carrier, and gives the learner an actionable ordering rule (lower box first) they can transfer to later time signatures.
scored.tutorAdaptationEvidence: Top number, bottom number, one bar, one beat unit. Read the lower box first: with 8 here, which card is the one beat unit? Then we ask how many of that unit the quarter-note tile covers, not what speed it sets.
scored.tutorAdaptationJustification: After L2's reframing of the 4 as naming the unit, the tutor immediately adapts by shifting the question order to 'Read the lower box first', introducing a new time signature, and explicitly discarding speed language, thereby taking up the learner's revised frame and routing the next task through it.

tutor_strategy_reversal.justification: In T2 the tutor shows stock-taking contrast by explicitly replacing 'which box did the bar line test' with 'Read the lower box first' and by ruling out speed-language, then introduces a new public mechanism: a changed object (eighth-note card and 8 in the lower box) and a shifted task (identify the beat unit from the lower number, then re-read the quarter-note as covering a count of that unit rather than setting speed).

tutor_adaptive_mechanism_quality.justification: The mechanism is precise and directly fitted to the learner's pressure on the lower number: it makes the beat unit a physical tile the learner selects, reframes the quarter-note as a tile that covers n beat-units rather than a speed carrier, and gives the learner an actionable ordering rule (lower box first) they can transfer to later time signatures.
